name: Ramen Vegetable Steak Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: Cut crosswise into one-inch wide strips
1 package (three ounces) Oriental Ramen noodle soup
1 package (16 ounces) Green Giant American Style Frozen Broccoli, Carrots, Water Chestnuts & Red peppers
1 Jar (four and a half ounces) Whole mushrooms, drained
recipeInstructions:
Soy sauce and ramen noodle soup flavor this quick entree.
In a medium-sized bowl, combine beef strips, garlic, one tablespoon of sesame oil, and ground red pepper. Toss to coat. Set aside.
Remove noodles from the package; reserve the seasoning packet. Bring water to a boil in a large saucepan. Break the block of noodles into three pieces and add to the water with the frozen vegetables. Return to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for two to three minutes, or until the vegetables are crisp-tender, stirring occasionally. Drain well.
Return the vegetable mixture to the saucepan and stir in the seasoning packet from the noodles. Cover and keep warm.
Rub one teaspoon of cooking oil over a large skillet or wok; heat over medium-high heat until hot. Add steak; cook and stir for one to two minutes, or until meat is no longer pink. Stir in the vegetable mixture, mushrooms, and soy sauce; heat thoroughly.
Garnish with green onions. Makes four (one-and-a-half cup servings).
Nutritional information: 1.5 cups per serving. 330 calories; 26g protein; 25g carbohydrate; 4g dietary fiber; 13g fat (polyunsaturated 2g; saturated 4g); Cholesterol 58mg; sodium 840mg; 620 mg potassium.
Dietary Exchanges: 1 Starch, 2.5 lean meat; 2 vegetables, and 1 fat.
